name: Double Chocolate, Chocolate Chip Sandwich Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup finely chopped pecans or walnuts
3/4 cup semisweet or milk chocolate chips or chunks
1/4 cup heavy cream, plus additional for whisking
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a small saucepan over low heat, melt butter and chocolate together.
3. Meanwhile, in a separate bowl, mix flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
4. Cool the melted chocolate mixture slightly and add it to the mixer bowl along with both sugars, vanilla extract, sour cream or buttermilk, and beaten egg. Mix until well combined.
5. Add the dry ingredients to the chocolate mixture and gently fold in pecans and chocolate chips until evenly distributed.
6. Grease cookie sheets with parchment paper or non-stick cooking spray.
7. For 3-inch round cookies: Drop rounded teaspoonfuls of batter onto the prepared sheet, spacing about 2 inches apart.
8. For 4-inch round cookies: Drop rounded tablespoonfuls of batter onto the prepared sheet, leaving about 2 inches between each cookie.
9. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes (3-inch) or 12 to 15 minutes (4-inch), until lightly golden around the edges but not browned.
10. Remove from oven and let cool on wire racks.
11. To make ganache filling: Melt chocolate and butter in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally. Gradually whisk in heavy cream until smooth. Let stand at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour, or until slightly thickened but still pourable.
12. Turn half the cooled cookies upside down on wax paper. Spread a spoonful of ganache on each cookie and quickly cover with the remaining cookies right sides up, pressing gently to spread filling evenly.
13. Allow sandwiches to cool completely before serving. Makes 20 small or 9 large sandwiches.
